title: Dark Matter
puzzle: Why do astronomers think most matter in the universe is invisible?
insight: Galaxies rotate and cluster as if much more mass exists than we can see. Scientists call that missing gravitational source dark matter.
key_ideas: Visible stars and gas do not supply enough gravity.
Dark matter is inferred from motion and lensing, not direct light.
It seems to help large cosmic structures stay together.
mind_blower: A huge part of the universe may shape everything around us while never glowing at all.
next_curiosity: If dark matter is invisible, what clues might finally reveal what it is?
narration_text: Why do astronomers think most matter in the universe is invisible? Galaxies rotate and cluster as if much more mass exists than we can see. Scientists call that missing gravitational source dark matter. Visible stars and gas do not supply enough gravity. Dark matter is inferred from motion and lensing, not direct light. It seems to help large cosmic structures stay together. A huge part of the universe may shape everything around us while never glowing at all. If dark matter is invisible, what clues might finally reveal what it is?
